_schema: feature-name -> { chain, txid, date }. A catalogue feature is 'verified' ONLY when a real, explorer-confirmed testnet or mainnet txid exists here. This file is the SOLE source of the 'verified' state; the catalogue reads it and nothing else writes it. Code inspection, passing tests, and clean review NEVER promote a feature to verified (see CLAUDE.md 'Verify, don't assert'). Empty = nothing on-chain-verified yet; every code-complete feature shows 'built' until a txid lands here.

_corrected.USDT mainnet send (full UI path, build:r: Txid corrected 2026-06-22. PR #280 originally recorded 0x3f2fe19a1092af36b4ca758846c4d266fd55f947efc110a1122013c2b242b986 — that hash is a real mainnet tx but targets the USDC contract, not USDT, so it did not substantiate the USDT claim. The actual USDT mainnet send is 0xf06a0ba731d1b8bf4d3f859a5904830b2f064725ba837c8c7332e5264f0b5b08 (verified on-chain via eth_getTransactionByHash/Receipt 2026-06-22: to=Tether USDT contract 0xdAC17F95…831ec7, transfer 1 USDT to 0x82D0…5BAB, chainId 1, status SUCCESS, block 25360159). Entry below now carries the correct txid.

_h1_passkey_2fa_fix_confirmation._why_not_in_evidence: Deliberately a META key, NOT under 'evidence' — so it does NOT promote any catalogue feature to 'verified'. The Two-Factor feature stays BUILT. This records that the ECC audit H-1 fix (passkey-2FA was silently bypassed on Send) was exercised end-to-end on-chain, while being honest that the authenticator was SOFTWARE, not a Secure Enclave.
_h1_passkey_2fa_fix_confirmation.finding: ECC audit H-1 — SendCrypto keyed the 2FA gate on actionPasswordConfigured alone, silently bypassing a passkey-only second factor. Fixed in PR #340 (resolveSend2faMethod).
_h1_passkey_2fa_fix_confirmation.what_was_verified: Fully-automated web e2e (Playwright + Chrome CDP virtual authenticator). Imported the real throwaway testnet seed (NOT demo), registered a passkey, enabled PIN+Passkey 2FA, and drove a real Sepolia ETH send through the full UI. The send screen rendered the TwoFactorGate ('Authorise this send with your PIN + passkey') — i.e. the passkey factor was ENFORCED, not bypassed — and broadcast ONLY after a genuine WebAuthn assertion (CDP credentialAsserted event; authenticator signCount 1->2).
_h1_passkey_2fa_fix_confirmation.verified_onchain: eth_getTransactionByHash + eth_getTransactionReceipt, chainId 11155111, status SUCCESS, gasUsed 21000, 2026-06-23
_h1_passkey_2fa_fix_confirmation.caveats: (1) The authenticator is a CDP virtual (software) authenticator, NOT a Secure Enclave — this confirms the H-1 WIRING (no silent bypass; the assertion genuinely gates the send), which is what H-1 is, but NOT the hardware-possession strength of the factor. A physical-device (iPhone Face ID / security key) passkey send is still the bar to flip Two-Factor from BUILT to 'verified'. (2) navigator.webdriver was masked to false so RASP did not block the test harness — touches RASP's automation probe only, never the 2FA path. Incidentally, with webdriver=true RASP DID block the send (blockedByRisk suppressed the gate), confirming RASP is wired into SendCrypto.

_ios_hardware_kek_device_verification._why_not_in_evidence: Deliberately a META key, NOT under 'evidence' — it does NOT promote any catalogue feature to 'verified'. iOS Hardware KEK (H-NEW-D) stays BUILT / device-verified (PARTIAL). Records that the real Secure Enclave ECIES KEK (PR #495) gated two real on-chain Sepolia sends on a physical iPhone, while being honest that the getHardwareFactor SE-unlock trace was not captured live and the biometric re-enrollment invalidation test + independent audit are outstanding.
_ios_hardware_kek_device_verification.what_was_verified: Real Objective-C SE-ECIES HardwareKekPlugin (SecKeyCreateEncryptedData/DecryptedData over a persistent Secure Enclave P-256 key, .biometryCurrentSet ACL, Face ID gates every decrypt; binary-confirmed superclass=CAPPlugin). Two real Sepolia ETH sends from a KEK-enrolled vault on iPhone 17 Pro Max, both SUCCESS on-chain. NOT demo (real wallet address + real on-chain balance change).
_ios_hardware_kek_device_verification.proof_basis: Architectural + enrollment: the vault had kekWrap present; the fail-closed native.js _unlockInner KEK path (~L188-215) cannot decrypt the seed — hence cannot sign — unless getHardwareFactor() returns valid H from the SE (Face ID) AND the correct PIN derives C. Two valid on-chain signatures from a KEK-enrolled vault therefore prove the SE-KEK unlock gated signing. This is architectural proof, NOT an observed getHardwareFactor log line.
_ios_hardware_kek_device_verification.verified_onchain: publicnode Sepolia RPC (eth_getTransactionByHash + eth_getTransactionReceipt), chainId 11155111, both status SUCCESS, 2026-07-01
_ios_hardware_kek_device_verification.caveats: (1) The live getHardwareFactor SE-unlock log trace tied to these sends was NOT captured — proof is architectural (fail-closed KEK path) + user-confirmed enrollment, not an observed SE-unlock line. (2) The biometric re-enrollment invalidation test (disable/re-enroll Face ID -> old SE key invalidated -> unlock re-prompts / password fallback) is NOT done. (3) UNAUDITED-PROVISIONAL — no independent audit. This is the KEK unlock gate, not an asset status (ETH is already LIVE). Android StrongBox equivalent: PR #496 (Pixel 10 Pro XL).

_c1_android_kek_v2_device_verification._why_not_in_evidence: Deliberately a META key, NOT under 'evidence' — it does NOT promote any catalogue feature to 'verified'. Records that the C-1 CRITICAL (Android HMAC fixed input) v2 protocol fix was end-to-end device-verified: a vault enrolled under the v2 kekSalt-bound HMAC protocol (hardwareKekVersion:2) gated a real on-chain Sepolia send on a Pixel 10 Pro XL. Android C-1 is RESOLVED / device-verified. Remaining open: iOS kekSalt binding (iOS uses ECIES not HMAC — separate design), independent audit.
_c1_android_kek_v2_device_verification.verified_onchain: eth_getTransactionByHash, chainId 11155111 (Sepolia), type 0x2 (EIP-1559), status confirmed (blockHash present), block 11185289, 2026-07-02.
_c1_android_kek_v2_device_verification.what_was_verified: C-1 v2 protocol end-to-end on Android. The vault was enrolled via enrollKek() with v2 code (PR #529, commit 732f9676): native.js generated a fresh 32-byte kekSalt, passed it to getHardwareFactor({ kekSalt }) — confirmed via nativePromise intercept (kekSalt Uint8Array arg present in two consecutive getHardwareFactor calls), and stamped hardwareKekVersion:2 + kekSalt on the vault blob (confirmed via SecureStorage.get() CDP read: hardwareKekVersion:2, kekSaltLength:44, hardwareKekTier:STRONGBOX). The vault was then force-killed, relaunched, and biometric-unlocked before signing and broadcasting this send. H = HMAC(StrongBox key, kekSalt) — unique per-enrollment, NOT the global fixed PRF_EVAL_SALT that C-1 identified as the vulnerability.
_c1_android_kek_v2_device_verification.apk: com.veyrnox.app.debug, versionCode=1, installed 2026-07-02 09:29:47, built from commit a55e5464 (post PR #529 merge at 06:33:05)
_c1_android_kek_v2_device_verification.caveats: (1) The KEK gates UNLOCK (H+C unwrap DEK → decrypt seed → sign tx), not per-signature. (2) INTERNAL verification — not independently audited. (3) iOS v2 equivalent (kekSalt binding via ECIES) is a separate design and remains unverified. (4) C-1 v2 PR #529 is merged (commit 732f9676); no open PRs for C-1 Android.

_android_hardware_kek_device_verificatio._why_not_in_evidence: Deliberately a META key, NOT under 'evidence' — it does NOT promote any catalogue feature to 'verified'. Android Hardware KEK (H-NEW-D / StrongBox) stays BUILT / device-verified (PARTIAL). Moved here from 'evidence' per the ECC KEK audit 2026-07-01 (finding L6) so it mirrors _ios_hardware_kek_device_verification and cannot become a latent promotion trap. Records that the StrongBox KEK gated a real on-chain Sepolia send on a Pixel 10 Pro XL, honest that this gates UNLOCK (not per-signature), StrongBox tier is observed not enforced, and biometric re-enrollment invalidation + independent audit are outstanding.
_android_hardware_kek_device_verificatio.verified_onchain: eth_getTransactionByHash + eth_getTransactionReceipt, chainId 11155111, status SUCCESS, block 11180398, 2026-07-01. Sent from a StrongBox-KEK-enrolled vault whose unlock was gated by the Android StrongBox KEK: device logcat (Pixel 10 Pro XL, com.veyrnox.app.debug) shows HardwareKek.getHardwareFactor + BiometricService StrengthRequested:15 (BIOMETRIC_STRONG, no credential fallback) and the vault reading back as kek-dek (kekWrap present) — the seed could only be decrypted, and thus this tx signed, after the StrongBox hardware factor H was produced.
_android_hardware_kek_device_verificatio.caveats: (1) The KEK gates UNLOCK (H + PIN unwrap the DEK); the send then signs with the in-memory DEK — this confirms the send REQUIRED the StrongBox KEK to unlock, not that a per-signature SE/StrongBox key signed the tx. (2) StrongBox tier is device-specific to this Pixel 10 Pro XL (securityLevel=2); a non-StrongBox device would log TRUSTED_ENVIRONMENT — tier ENFORCEMENT is still TARGET. (3) BUILT + device-verified, NOT independently audited. (4) Depends on the 3-bug fix (PRs #497/#499) that made the KEK actually persist + gate unlock — before that the vault was silently downgraded to bare on every unlock, so no earlier Android send was genuinely KEK-gated. (5) Biometric re-enrollment invalidation test (old key invalidated after re-enroll) PASSED on this device (PR #516/#518) — see the _hardware_kek_biometric_reenroll_invalidation META key.

_hardware_kek_biometric_reenroll_invalid._why_not_in_evidence: Deliberately a META key, NOT under 'evidence' — it does NOT promote any catalogue feature to 'verified'. This is an on-device PROTECTION test (biometric re-enrollment invalidation), not a testnet txid, so it must stay out of the txid-gated 'evidence' set (featureCatalogue.verifiedFeatureNames = Object.keys(evidence)). Mirrors _ios/_android_hardware_kek_device_verification. Records that setInvalidatedByBiometricEnrollment was verified working on a Pixel 10 Pro XL (I6 hardware-binding property). iOS equivalent still outstanding (device Face ID enrollment was restricted on the test iPhone).
_hardware_kek_biometric_reenroll_invalid.procedure: 1. Enrolled KEK (biometric + StrongBox). 2. Deleted one OS fingerprint. 3. Re-enrolled new fingerprint (triggers biometric state change). 4. Force-closed app + cold restart. 5. Attempted unlock.
_hardware_kek_biometric_reenroll_invalid.result: ✅ PASS — unlock attempt returned 'Hardware key invalidated - re-enrollment required' error. The OS invalidated the KEK key (via setInvalidatedByBiometricEnrollment), the app detected it, and refused an insecure unlock (fail-closed, I4).
_hardware_kek_biometric_reenroll_invalid.proof: Device logcat + on-device error message. Confirmed PIN fallback worked (recovery path intact).
_hardware_kek_biometric_reenroll_invalid.verified_ondevice: Pixel 10 Pro XL, com.veyrnox.app.debug, 2026-07-01T16:30 UTC. Biometric invalidation protection (setInvalidatedByBiometricEnrollment) is WORKING — key invalidates on biometric state change, app detects and refuses unlock, fail-closed. Recovery (PIN fallback) successful.

_ios_kek_se_operation_os_evidence._why_not_in_evidence: Deliberately a META key, NOT under 'evidence' — it does NOT promote any catalogue feature to 'verified'. iOS Hardware KEK stays BUILT / device-verified (PARTIAL). Records OS-daemon (coreauthd/biometrickitd/ctkd) evidence that the Veyrnox app performed a Face-ID-gated Secure Enclave key operation correlated in time to a real on-chain Sepolia send. It is NOT the app's own getHardwareFactor SUCCESS log line and does NOT by itself close iOS-F9's literal app-trace requirement (see caveats).
_ios_kek_se_operation_os_evidence.test_type: real-device OS-daemon log correlation (no reinstall; existing KEK-enrolled vault)
_ios_kek_se_operation_os_evidence.app_pid: 4913 (com.veyrnox.app) — bound in ctkd token-client.peer[4913] and coreauthd ContextProxy[4913:...]
_ios_kek_se_operation_os_evidence.verified_onchain: publicnode Sepolia RPC eth_getTransactionByHash, chainId 11155111, block 11185985, nonce 32, from 0x90f9…E68a729, 2026-07-02.
_ios_kek_se_operation_os_evidence.os_evidence: Device unified-log window 2026-07-02 09:29–10:09 (log collect --device-udid, read via log show — reading needs no root). At 10:06:2x coreauthd logged two Face ID matches (BKMatchOperation::matchResult via the Pearl camera) then allowTransferToProcess:269 on ContextProxy[4913:38:1712:1818] and evaluateACL … returned — a SecAccessControl ACL evaluation gating a key operation for the Veyrnox process. ctkd (CryptoTokenKit — the Secure Enclave broker) held a token-client.peer[4913] connection from 09:57:09 through 10:07:25 and created the gating LAContext[…:1712:1818] at 10:06:24. Veyrnox's only Secure Enclave key is the HardwareKek ECIES key, so a ctkd/SE key operation from pid 4913 is the KEK getHardwareFactor decrypt. The SE operation is timestamped immediately before the nonce-32 send.
_ios_kek_se_operation_os_evidence.proof_basis: Independent Apple security-daemon logs (coreauthd/biometrickitd/ctkd), not the app — the app cannot forge them. Establishes that Veyrnox performed a Face-ID-gated Secure Enclave CryptoTokenKit key operation correlated in time to a real on-chain send. Stronger than a self-reported app log line for authenticity, weaker for explicit labelling (see caveats).
_ios_kek_se_operation_os_evidence.caveats: (1) This is NOT the app's own '[VEYRNOX-KEK] getHardwareFactor: SUCCESS' NSLog line. On iOS 26 a third-party app's NSLog is not persisted to / streamable from the device unified log by external tools — idevicesyslog, retroactive log collect, AND live Console.app all showed zero VEYRNOX-KEK lines despite Face ID running and the SE op firing. The literal app trace cannot be captured from the installed build; it would require a fresh debug rebuild logging via os_log(public). (2) coreauthd redacts some client bindings as <private>; the ctkd peer[4913] and coreauthd ContextProxy[4913:…] bindings are explicit and unredacted. (3) iOS-F9's literal app-trace requirement therefore remains OPEN — this entry is OS-side corroboration that upgrades the prior architectural-only proof, NOT a literal close. (4) UNAUDITED — no independent audit. The KEK gates UNLOCK, not asset status (ETH is already LIVE).

Two-Factor — Face ID biometric possessio.verified_onchain: Explorer-confirmed 2026-06-29: status SUCCESS, chainId 11155111, block 11168258, 51 confirmations. Send was gated behind Face ID (OS biometric possession factor) on a physical iOS device — NOT a software authenticator. This is the physical-device bar stated in _h1_passkey_2fa_fix_confirmation caveats.
